2605.30280.title: Qwen-VLA: Unifying Vision-Language-Action Modeling across Tasks, Environments, and Robot Embodiments
2605.30280.paper_summary: Embodied intelligence is often studied through specialized models for individual tasks such as manipulation or navigation, resulting in fragmented capabilities and limited generalization across tasks, environments, and robot embodiments. In this work, we study whether heterogeneous embodied decision-making problems can be unified within a single vision-language-action model. We present Qwen-VLA, a unified embodied foundation model that extends Qwen's vision-language modeling stack from perception, understanding, and reasoning to continuous action and trajectory generation through a DiT-based action decoder. Qwen-VLA is trained with a large-scale joint pretraining recipe over diverse data sources, including robotics manipulation trajectories, human egocentric demonstrations, synthetic simulation data, vision-and-language navigation data, trajectory-centric supervision, and auxiliary vision-language data. To support multiple robot platforms, we introduce embodiment-aware prompt conditioning, where robot-specific textual descriptions specify the current embodiment and control convention. We further cast manipulation, navigation, and trajectory prediction into a unified action-and-trajectory prediction framework, enabling transferable visual grounding, spatial reasoning, and continuous action generation across robot morphologies, task families, and environments. Experiments on manipulation, navigation, and trajectory-centric benchmarks show consistent multi-task performance and out-of-distribution generalization under variations in scene layout, background, lighting, object configuration, and robot embodiment. Qwen-VLA-Instruct achieves 97.9% on LIBERO, 73.7% on Simpler-WidowX, 86.1%/87.2% on RoboTwin-Easy/Hard, 69.0% OSR on R2R, 59.6% SR on RxR, 76.9% average OOD success in real-world ALOHA experiments, and 26.6% zero-shot success on DOMINO dynamic manipulation.
2605.30280.paper_summary_zh: 具身智能通常通過針對個別任務（如操控或導航）的專門模型進行研究，這導致能力的碎片化以及在任務、環境和機器人具身之間的有限泛化。在本研究中，我們探討異質具身決策問題是否可以統一在單一的視覺-語言-行動模型中。我們提出了Qwen-VLA，一個統一的具身基礎模型，它擴展了Qwen的視覺-語言建模堆棧，從感知、理解和推理擴展到通過基於DiT的行動解碼器進行連續行動和軌跡生成。Qwen-VLA使用大規模的聯合預訓練配方在多樣化的數據來源上進行訓練，包括機器人操控軌跡、人類自我中心的演示、合成模擬數據、視覺與語言導航數據、以軌跡為中心的監督和輔助的視覺-語言數據。為了支持多個機器人平台，我們引入了具身感知的提示條件，其中機器人特定的文本描述指定當前的具身和控制約定。我們進一步將操控、導航和軌跡預測轉化為統一的行動和軌跡預測框架，使得在機器人形態、任務家族和環境之間能夠進行可轉移的視覺基礎、空間推理和連續行動生成。在操控、導航和以軌跡為中心的基準測試中的實驗顯示，在場景佈局、背景、照明、物體配置和機器人具身的變化下，表現出一致的多任務性能和超出分佈的泛化。Qwen-VLA-Instruct在LIBERO上達到97.9%、在Simpler-WidowX上達到73.7%、在RoboTwin-Easy/Hard上達到86.1%/87.2%、在R2R上達到69.0% OSR、在RxR上達到59.6% SR、在現實世界的ALOHA實驗中達到76.9%的平均OOD成功率，以及在DOMINO動態操控上達到26.6%的零樣本成功率。
2605.30280.author: Qiuyue Wang et.al.
